description: Linux: FS [/]: Running out of free inodes
comments: Disk writing may fail if index nodes are exhausted, leading to error messages like "No space left on device" or "Disk is full", despite available free space.
event_name: Linux: /: Running out of free inodes (free < {$VFS.FS.INODE.PFREE.MIN.CRIT:"/"}%)

description: Linux: FS [/]: Running out of free inodes
comments: Disk writing may fail if index nodes are exhausted, leading to error messages like "No space left on device" or "Disk is full", despite available free space.
event_name: Linux: FS [/]: Running out of free inodes (free < {$VFS.FS.INODE.PFREE.MIN.WARN:"/"}%)

description: Linux: FS [/]: Space is critically low
comments:
The volume's space usage exceeds the `{$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.CRIT:"/"}%` limit.
The trigger expression is based on the current used and maximum available spaces.
Event name represents the total volume space, which can differ from the maximum available space, depending on the filesystem type.
event_name: Linux: FS [/]: Space is critically low (used > {$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.CRIT:"/"}%, total {{?last(//vfs.fs.dependent.size[/,total])/1024/1024/1024}.fmtnum(1)}GB)

description: Linux: FS [/]: Space is low
comments:
The volume's space usage exceeds the `{$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.WARN:"/"}%` limit.
The trigger expression is based on the current used and maximum available spaces.
Event name represents the total volume space, which can differ from the maximum available space, depending on the filesystem type.
event_name: Linux: FS [/]: Space is low (used > {$VFS.FS.PUSED.MAX.WARN:"/"}%, total {{?last(//vfs.fs.dependent.size[/,total])/1024/1024/1024}.fmtnum(1)}GB)

description: Linux: FS [/]: Filesystem has become read-only
comments: The filesystem has become read-only, possibly due to an I/O error. Available only for Zabbix agents 6.4 and higher.

description: Cert [souljourneys.space]: Fingerprint has changed
comments:
The SSL certificate fingerprint has changed. If you did not update the certificate, it may mean your certificate has been hacked. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.
There could be multiple valid certificates on some installations. In this case, the trigger will have a false positive. You can ignore it or disable the trigger.
error: Cannot evaluate function last(/sjops.souljourneys.space/cert.sha1_fingerprint[souljourneys.space],#2): not enough data.
event_name: Cert [souljourneys.space]: Fingerprint has changed (new version: {ITEM.VALUE})

Linux: Interface eth0: High error rate
comments: It recovers when it is below 80% of the `{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"eth0"}` threshold.
event_name: Linux: Interface eth0: High error rate (>{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"eth0"} for 5m)

description: Linux: Interface enp7s0: High error rate
comments: It recovers when it is below 80% of the `{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"enp7s0"}` threshold.
event_name: Linux: Interface enp7s0: High error rate (>{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"enp7s0"} for 5m)

description: Linux: Interface eth0: High bandwidth usage
comments: The utilization of the network interface is close to its estimated maximum bandwidth.
error: Cannot evaluate function last(/sjops.souljourneys.space/vfs.file.contents["/sys/class/net/eth0/speed"]): item is not supported.
event_name: Linux: Interface eth0: High bandwidth usage (>{$IF.UTIL.MAX:"eth0"}%)

description: Linux: Interface enp7s0: High bandwidth usage
comments: The utilization of the network interface is close to its estimated maximum bandwidth.
error: Cannot evaluate function last(/sjops.souljourneys.space/vfs.file.contents["/sys/class/net/enp7s0/speed"]): item is not supported.
event_name: Linux: Interface enp7s0: High bandwidth usage (>{$IF.UTIL.MAX:"enp7s0"}%)

description: Linux: Interface eth0: Ethernet has changed to lower speed than it was before
comments: This Ethernet connection has transitioned down from its known maximum speed. This might be a sign of autonegotiation issues. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.
error: Cannot evaluate function change(/sjops.souljourneys.space/vfs.file.contents["/sys/class/net/eth0/speed"]): item is not supported.

description: Linux: Interface enp7s0: Ethernet has changed to lower speed than it was before
comments: This Ethernet connection has transitioned down from its known maximum speed. This might be a sign of autonegotiation issues. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.
error: Cannot evaluate function change(/sjops.souljourneys.space/vfs.file.contents["/sys/class/net/enp7s0/speed"]): item is not supported.

description: Linux: Interface eth0: Link down
comments:
This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"eth0"}=1` - a user can redefine the context macro to "0", marking this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#1)<>last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#2)` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, does not fire for "eternal off" interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff.

description: Linux: Interface enp7s0: Link down
comments:
This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"enp7s0"}=1` - a user can redefine the context macro to "0", marking this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#1)<>last(/TEMPLATE_NAME/METRIC,#2)` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, does not fire for "eternal off" interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff.

description: Linux: sda: Disk read/write request responses are too high
comments: This trigger might indicate the disk `sda` saturation.
event_name: Linux: sda: Disk read/write request responses are too high (read > {$VFS.DEV.READ.AWAIT.WARN:"sda"} ms for 15m or write > {$VFS.DEV.WRITE.AWAIT.WARN:"sda"} ms for 15m)
